message:
fix(reactor): lab reactor poll advances time to min(deadline, next_event) and delivers HUP from closed flag

Lab reactor poll now computes target_time as the minimum of the timeout
deadline and the next pending event time, instead of blindly adding the
full timeout duration. When the reactor was woken (via inject_readiness
or wake()), time stays at the current value. This prevents time from
advancing past events that should fire at intermediate timestamps.

inject_close no longer schedules a synthetic HUP event in the pending
queue. Instead, the poll loop checks the closed flag on each socket and
delivers HUP directly during event processing. This fixes a race where
the synthetic HUP could be delivered before readiness events that were
queued earlier, and eliminates stale HUP events that linger after a
socket is re-opened.

The woken flag uses swap with AcqRel ordering instead of load+store to
prevent a TOCTOU race where a wake between the load and the store would
be lost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
